title: CDN Serving Stale Cached Content After Backend Update
symptoms: Product prices on the website not matching backend database values
CDN cache headers showing content served from stale cache
CloudFront invalidation request in failed state
Deployment script logging IAM credential error for invalidation API
Different pricing shown to users in different regions based on edge cache age
root_cause: CDN cache invalidation failed silently during deployment because the IAM service account credentials used by the deployment script had expired
resolution: Rotated IAM credentials, triggered manual CloudFront cache invalidation for all product-related paths, verified fresh content being served from all edge locations
preventive_actions: Implement IAM credential rotation monitoring with expiry alerts
Add cache invalidation verification step in deployment pipeline
Create automated cache freshness testing after deployments
Implement cache-busting versioned URLs for dynamic content
Add deployment pipeline gate requiring successful invalidation before completion

timeline.event: Cache invalidation request fails silently due to expired IAM credentials
First customer report of incorrect pricing
Support tickets accumulating, pricing discrepancy pattern identified
Sarah Chen investigates, discovers failed invalidation in CloudFront
Expired IAM credentials identified as root cause
IAM credentials rotated, manual cache invalidation triggered
All edge locations serving fresh content, incident resolved

embedding_summary: CloudFront CDN serving stale product pricing for 2 hours after backend update because cache invalidation failed due to expired IAM credentials. 340 orders placed with incorrect pricing. Resolved by rotating IAM credentials and triggering manual cache invalidation.
